item.title: Complaint about road sweepers in Manor Rd Bexley today, 2nd April
item.description:
Complaint about road sweepers in Manor Rd Bexley today - caller witnessed the road sweepers park up in Manor Rd, lady says that they scraped off some soil from a wall o/s 1 North Cray Rd and then drove off. Lady says they should have been sweeping Manor Rd/Vicarage Rd/North Cray Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): North Cray Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Footpath entrance off Willesden Lane, 2nd April

item.title: Human Waste, 2nd April
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 02/04/26 12:26 • Please give brief details of your complaint or compliment below: I live in Harwood Avenue on the turning circle adjacent to Kentish Way. We are expereiencing an increase in human urination and defication in the bushes in the turning circle and against the walls of our houses. This is an all day occurance and when you politely ask for the person not to do this they are generally rude and offensive. The number of times this is happening is now become a health hazzard and is having an affect on my and my neighbours day. I really dont need to see so many penis' everyday, it is offensive. We have young children living in close proximity to this behaviour. The police SNT are aware of this, however there is not much they can do as they have to witness the behaviour and they do not have the resources to place an officer there all day. • What action (if any) would you like us to take? We would like visable cctv and sineage put up in the road. We woul also like the bushes to be reinsated in the turning circle to stop the people from being able to acces the areas to relieve themselves. • Have you already spoken to somebody in the Council about this matter? yes • If yes, and you know who you spoke to please provide details and dates: Sam Webber arranged for the council and ID verde to visit a gentleman called David from the council and two ladies from the council were there. We were advised fhat bushes would be reisataed in November, thisbis yet to happen.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harwood Avenue, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Worst pothole I’ve ever had, 2nd April

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): North Circular Road, Brent Cross, Greater London

item.title: Somebody is parking in the green zone, 2nd April
